perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Attachments A, G or H, neither party can suspend its further performance, terminate this Agreement or require specific performance of the other party of this Agreement in whole or in part as a result of the other party's material breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement without first providing notice to such party in writing and thirty (30) days' opportunity to cure the material breach (and then only if such party fails to cure such breach).
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Attachments A, G or H, either party can suspend its further performance, terminate this Agreement or require specific performance of the other party of this Agreement immediately and in whole or in part as a result of the other party's material breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement without providing prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires a 30-day cure period before termination for a material breach, while the modified text allows immediate termination without prior notice. This creates a contradiction regarding the process for addressing breaches of the agreement. This directly contradicts the original intent of allowing a cure period, introducing uncertainty in enforcement and potentially leading to legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Attachments A, G or H, either party can suspend its further performance, terminate this Agreement or require specific performance of the other party of this Agreement immediately and in whole or in part as a result of the other party's material breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement without providing prior notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires a 30-day cure period for a material breach before termination. The changed text allows immediate termination without prior notice, contradicting the original cure period provision. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, so the core issue is whether the change introduced a contradiction, which it did. The original text created an obligation to wait 30 days. The changed text removed this obligation, therefore contradicting the previous requirement. This falls under structural flaws, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. The modified clause in the changed text directly conflicts with the intent and established process outlined in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer Affiliates shall pay ExxonMobil's Selling Affiliates invoice(s) not later than the days set forth in Attachment E hereto. All invoices shall be paid in full by wire transfer in accordance with the invoice's instructions.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer Affiliates may, at their discretion, pay ExxonMobil's Selling Affiliates invoice(s) at any point after the days set forth in Attachment E hereto, or not at all. Invoices may or may not be paid in full, and the method of payment is not restricted to wire transfer.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes a clear payment deadline and method. The modified text allows Buyer Affiliates to disregard the payment terms specified in Attachment E, and allows for non or delayed payments, and changes the payment method. This directly contradicts the original and legally binding commitment to timely and complete payment, therefore creating uncertainty and potential non-compliance, that are mentioned in Attachment E, by Buyer Affiliates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer Affiliates may, at their discretion, pay ExxonMobil's Selling Affiliates invoice(s) at any point after the days set forth in Attachment E hereto, or not at all. Invoices may or may not be paid in full, and the method of payment is not restricted to wire transfer. Crude Trigger Clause Product price(s) shall be subject to the Average Brent crude oil price evolution (as further detailed below) in order to reflect the cost of energy. Should the Average Brent crude oil price at any moment during the term of this Agreement move to a different Average Brent crude oil price bracket as mentioned below, Seller may increase or decrease the Product price by $[*****] for every $[*****] change in the Average Brent crude oil price. In no event shall the Product price's increase or decrease exceed $[*****] for every $[*****] change in the Average Brent crude oil price brackets. Each Average Brent crude oil bracket is calculated on a $5 range basis (e.g., $30-$35, $40-$45, $50-$55, etc.). The Average Brent crude oil price evolution shall be expressed as the three-month average spot price per barrel of the Brent crude Source: WEST PHARMACEUTICAL SERVICES INC, 8-K, 1/16/2020 oil (as published in the Wall Street Journal). This average shall be calculated as the average of the prices for the immediate preceding three consecutive calendar months, with each month's price calculated as the average of the daily prices. Any conversion between United States Dollars and Euros will be carried out by using the average of the European Central Bank's daily foreign exchange rate as published in Reuter's screen ECB 37 for the period in question. Any Product price adjustment shall take effect the month immediately following Seller's notification of an increase or decrease in Product price.
perturbation.justification: The changed text permits Buyer Affiliates to disregard payment deadlines and methods specified in Attachment E and the original text. It allows for non-payment, delayed payment, and alternative payment methods. This directly contradicts the original, legally binding commitment to timely and complete payment by wire transfer, creating uncertainty and potential non-compliance. Because the changed text makes the payment terms optional, it directly clashes with the understanding that the Buyer Affiliates are obligated to pay under specific terms.
